seoTitle: IXYS Products | Power MOSFETs, IGBTs, Thyristors, Rectifiers | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse IXYS power semiconductor products including MOSFETs, IGBTs, thyristors, rectifiers, and power modules. Technical support available for industrial and automotive applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does IXYS offer?
faqs.answer: IXYS offers four main product categories: Power MOSFETs including high-voltage and high-current options for switching applications; IGBTs for high-power motor drives and inverters; Thyristors & Rectifiers including SCRs, triacs, and high-voltage rectifier diodes; and Power Modules integrating multiple devices for complete power conversion solutions. Each category covers a wide range of voltage and current ratings to meet diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IXYS product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right IXYS product involves: (1) Identify voltage requirements - Determine maximum blocking voltage needed. (2) Determine current rating - Calculate RMS and peak current requirements. (3) Consider switching frequency - MOSFETs for high frequency, IGBTs for lower frequency. (4) Evaluate thermal requirements - Check thermal resistance and power dissipation. (5) Review package options - TO-220, TO-247, modules, etc. (6) Check application notes - IXYS provides detailed selection guides. (7) Contact FAE - Our team can provide personalized recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of IXYS high-voltage power devices?
faqs.answer: IXYS high-voltage devices provide several advantages: (1) Wide voltage range - Devices available up to 4500V. (2) High reliability - Proven in harsh industrial environments. (3) Low leakage current - Excellent blocking characteristics. (4) Fast switching - Optimized for high-frequency applications. (5) Rugged design - High surge current capability. (6) Industry expertise - Over 35 years of high-voltage technology leadership. (7) Application support - Comprehensive technical documentation and FAE support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IXYS for high-voltage applications above 1200V or demanding industrial environments.

categories.name: Power MOSFETs
categories.description: IXYS power MOSFETs offer high-performance switching for power conversion applications with voltage ratings from 40V to 1500V.
categories.longDescription: IXYS power MOSFETs are designed for high-efficiency power conversion applications. The portfolio includes devices with voltage ratings from 40V to 1500V and current ratings up to hundreds of amps. IXYS MOSFETs feature low on-resistance, fast switching speeds, and rugged avalanche capability. Available in through-hole and surface-mount packages, these devices serve applications including switching power supplies, motor drives, DC-DC converters, and automotive systems. The company's expertise in high-voltage technology enables MOSFETs with exceptional performance at 600V and above. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: High-Voltage MOSFETs
series.description: 600V to 1500V devices for industrial and renewable energy

series.name: Low-Voltage MOSFETs
series.description: 40V to 250V devices for DC-DC and automotive

selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting MOSFETs for your application

faeReview.content: Excellent power mosfets component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What is the maximum operating temperature for IXFH40N60P3?
faqs.answer: IXFH40N60P3 temperature specifications: (1) Operating junction temperature - -55°C to +150°C. (2) Storage temperature - -55°C to +150°C. (3) Maximum case temperature - 100°C for continuous operation. (4) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.35°C/W typical. (5) For 40A operation, ensure case temperature stays below 100°C. (6) Use adequate heatsinking for high-current applications. (7) Thermal derating may be required above 25°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design thermal management to keep Tj below 125°C for long-term reliability.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in IXFH40N60P3?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation calculation for IXFH40N60P3: (1) Conduction loss: Pcond = I² × Rds(on) × duty cycle. At 20A with 0.085Ω and 50% duty: Pcond = 400 × 0.085 × 0.5 = 17W. (2) Switching loss: Psw = (Eon + Eoff) × fsw. At 50kHz with 0.5mJ each: Psw = 1mJ × 50kHz = 50W. (3) Total loss = 67W at 50kHz. (4) For lower frequencies, conduction loss dominates. (5) Use thermal simulation for accurate analysis. (6) Verify junction temperature with calculated losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate losses at worst-case conditions; design cooling accordingly.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for IXFH40N60P3?
faqs.answer: Gate drive recommendations for IXFH40N60P3: (1) Gate-source voltage - ±30V maximum rating. (2) Recommended drive - +10V to +15V for turn-on. (3) Threshold voltage - 3-5V typical. (4) Gate charge - 63nC total at 10V. (5) Gate resistance - 5-22Ω depending on switching speed. (6) Drive power - Approximately 3W at 50kHz. (7) Use low-impedance gate drive for fast switching. The device can be driven directly by most PWM controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +12V gate drive with 10Ω gate resistor for optimal performance.

faqs.question: Can IXFH40N60P3 be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXFH40N60P3 is excellent for solar inverters: (1) Voltage rating - 600V suitable for 400V DC bus systems. (2) Current rating - 40A supports inverters up to 5kW. (3) Low Rds(on) - Minimizes conduction losses for high efficiency. (4) Fast switching - Enables high-frequency operation. (5) Rugged design - Reliable in outdoor environments. (6) Cost-effective - Competitive pricing for commercial inverters. (7) Availability - Good stock and short lead times. Recommended for single-phase and small three-phase solar inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for solar inverters 3-5kW; parallel for higher power.

faqs.question: What is the avalanche rating of IXFH40N60P3?
faqs.answer: IXFH40N60P3 avalanche characteristics: (1) Single pulse avalanche energy - 500mJ at 25°C starting temperature. (2) Repetitive avalanche - Rated for limited repetitive operation. (3) UIS capability - Unclamped inductive switching rated. (4) Robustness - Designed to survive abnormal operating conditions. (5) Protection - Avalanche capability protects against voltage transients. (6) Applications - Suitable for inductive load switching. (7) Design margin - Include safety margin below rated avalanche energy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Device can survive limited avalanche; use snubbers for repetitive conditions.

faqs.question: How does Rds(on) vary with temperature in IXFH40N60P3?
faqs.answer: Rds(on) temperature characteristics: (1) At 25°C - 0.085Ω maximum. (2) At 125°C - Approximately 1.8x to 2x the 25°C value. (3) Positive temperature coefficient - Resistance increases with temperature. (4) Conduction loss - Increases at high temperature. (5) Parallel operation - Positive tempco enables current sharing. (6) Thermal design - Account for increased Rds(on) at high temperature. (7) Calculation - Use 0.15-0.17Ω for 125°C operation in loss calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for worst-case Rds(on) at maximum operating temperature.

faqs.question: What applications is IXFH80N50Q3 best suited for?
faqs.answer: IXFH80N50Q3 optimal applications: (1) Motor drives - High-current drives up to 10kW. (2) Welding equipment - Inverter welding power supplies. (3) DC-DC converters - High-power battery chargers. (4) Power supplies - Server and telecom power. (5) Solar inverters - High-power string inverters. (6) EV charging - DC fast charging stations. (7) Industrial automation - High-power motor control. The 500V rating is ideal for 380V AC systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-current applications 40-80A requiring low conduction losses.

faqs.question: How do I parallel IXFH80N50Q3 MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Paralleling IXFH80N50Q3 guidelines: (1) Static sharing - Positive tempco helps balance current. (2) Gate drive - Use common driver with individual gate resistors (2-5Ω). (3) Layout - Symmetrical layout with equal trace lengths. (4) Current imbalance - Expect 10-20% variation without matching. (5) Derating - Use 80% of combined rating. (6) Testing - Verify current sharing with oscilloscope. (7) Applications - Parallel 2-4 devices for very high current. The positive temperature coefficient makes paralleling easier.
faqs.decisionGuide: Parallel for currents above 80A; use symmetric layout and individual gate resistors.

faqs.question: What is the switching performance of IXFH80N50Q3?
faqs.answer: IXFH80N50Q3 switching characteristics: (1) Turn-on delay - 25ns typical. (2) Rise time - 35ns typical. (3) Turn-off delay - 65ns typical. (4) Fall time - 30ns typical. (5) Total gate charge - 110nC at 10V. (6) Input capacitance - 5200pF typical. (7) Switching losses - Lower at moderate frequencies (<100kHz). The device is optimized for high-efficiency switching at frequencies up to 200kHz.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimized for 50-150kHz operation; higher frequencies possible with proper gate drive.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for IXFH80N50Q3?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for IXFH80N50Q3: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.2°C/W. (2) At 60A with 0.042Ω: Pcond = 151W. (3) Required heatsink: Rth(s-a) < 0.3°C/W for 50°C ambient. (4) Forced air cooling recommended for continuous high current. (5) Thermal interface - Use high-quality thermal grease. (6) Mounting torque - 1.0-1.3 N·m for TO-247. (7) Temperature monitoring - Implement overtemperature protection. Liquid cooling may be needed for continuous 80A operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air or liquid cooling for continuous high-current operation.

faqs.question: Can IXFH80N50Q3 be used for welding machine applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXFH80N50Q3 is excellent for welding machines: (1) Current capability - 80A supports high-power welding. (2) Voltage rating - 500V suitable for 380V input systems. (3) Ruggedness - Designed for harsh welding environment. (4) Efficiency - Low Rds(on) minimizes heat generation. (5) Surge current - Handles welding starting transients. (6) Reliability - Proven in industrial welding equipment. (7) Cost - Competitive for welding power supply applications. Recommended for MMA, MIG/MAG, and TIG inverter welders.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for inverter welding power supplies 5-15kW output.

faqs.question: What protection features should be implemented with IXFH80N50Q3?
faqs.answer: Recommended protection for IXFH80N50Q3: (1) Overcurrent protection - Fast current limiting or shutdown. (2) Overvoltage protection - TVS or snubber for transients. (3) Overtemperature protection - Monitor heatsink temperature. (4) Gate overvoltage - Clamp gate voltage to ±20V. (5) dv/dt protection - Snubber for inductive loads. (6) Short-circuit protection - Fast desaturation detection. (7) ESD protection - Handle with ESD precautions during assembly. Implement comprehensive protection for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement all protection features for industrial applications; fast overcurrent is critical.

categories.name: IGBTs
categories.description: IXYS IGBTs provide high-efficiency power switching for motor drives, inverters, and power supplies with voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V.
categories.longDescription: IXYS IGBT portfolio includes discrete IGBTs and IGBT modules designed for high-power applications. Voltage ratings range from 600V to 1700V, serving applications from industrial motor drives to renewable energy systems. IXYS IGBTs feature low Vce(sat), fast switching characteristics, and excellent short-circuit withstand capability. The devices are available in various packages including TO-220, TO-247, and modules for different power levels. With advanced trench-gate technology, IXYS IGBTs deliver industry-leading efficiency and reliability. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Trench IGBTs
series.description: Advanced trench-gate technology for highest efficiency

selectionGuide.title: How to Select IXYS IGBTs
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting IGBTs for your power application

faqs.question: What is the difference between IXYS trench IGBT and planar IGBT?
faqs.answer: Trench vs planar IGBT comparison: (1) Structure - Trench has vertical channel, planar has horizontal. (2) Cell density - Trench achieves higher cell density. (3) Vce(sat) - Trench typically 0.3-0.5V lower. (4) Switching - Trench generally switches faster. (5) Ruggedness - Planar often more rugged for short-circuit. (6) Cost - Trench slightly higher due to process complexity. (7) Applications - Trench preferred for high-efficiency drives. The IXGH40N60B3D1 uses trench technology for best efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose trench IGBT for highest efficiency; planar for maximum ruggedness.

faqs.question: How do I calculate switching losses for IXGH40N60B3D1?
faqs.answer: Switching loss calculation for IXGH40N60B3D1: (1) Turn-on energy Eon - 0.8mJ typical at 20A, 300V. (2) Turn-off energy Eoff - 0.6mJ typical at same conditions. (3) Total switching energy - 1.4mJ per switching cycle. (4) Switching loss Psw = Etotal × fsw. At 20kHz: Psw = 1.4mJ × 20kHz = 28W. (5) Conduction loss Pcond = Vce(sat) × Ic × duty. At 20A with 1.9V: Pcond = 38W at 100% duty. (6) Total loss depends on duty cycle and frequency. (7) Use datasheet curves for accurate calculations at operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate both conduction and switching losses; optimize frequency for your application.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for IXGH40N60B3D1?
faqs.answer: Gate drive recommendations for IXGH40N60B3D1: (1) Gate-emitter voltage - ±20V maximum rating. (2) Recommended Vge - +15V for turn-on, -5V to -8V for turn-off. (3) Threshold voltage - 5.5V typical. (4) Gate charge - 63nC total at 15V. (5) Gate resistor - 10-47Ω depending on switching speed. (6) Drive power - Approximately 1.9W at 20kHz. (7) Miller plateau - At 7-8V during switching. Use isolated gate driver with desaturation protection for best results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V/-8V gate drive with 22Ω gate resistor for balanced switching.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand capability of IXGH40N60B3D1?
faqs.answer: IXGH40N60B3D1 short-circuit specifications: (1) Withstand time - 10μs minimum at 360V, 25°C. (2) Test conditions - Hard switching fault at rated voltage. (3) Protection - Gate drive must detect and shutdown within 10μs. (4) Desaturation detection - Recommended protection method. (5) Soft shutdown - Use soft turn-off to limit voltage spikes. (6) Repetitive capability - Not rated for repetitive short-circuit. (7) Design margin - Design protection for <5μs response time. The 10μs rating provides adequate time for protection circuits to respond.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement desaturation detection with <5μs response; use soft shutdown.

faqs.question: Can IXGH40N60B3D1 be used for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXGH40N60B3D1 is excellent for motor drives: (1) Voltage rating - 600V suitable for 380V AC motor drives. (2) Current rating - 40A supports motors up to 7.5kW. (3) Vce(sat) - Low 1.9V minimizes conduction losses. (4) Switching speed - Fast switching enables 16kHz carrier frequency. (5) Short-circuit ruggedness - 10μs withstand protects against faults. (6) Diode - Integrated fast recovery diode for freewheeling. (7) Reliability - Proven in industrial motor drive applications. Recommended for general-purpose VFDs and servo drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 3-7.5kW motor drives; excellent balance of performance and cost.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for IXGH40N60B3D1?
faqs.answer: IXGH40N60B3D1 frequency limitations: (1) Practical maximum - 40-50kHz with adequate cooling. (2) Recommended range - 5-20kHz for motor drives. (3) Loss trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses significantly. (4) At 40kHz - Switching losses approximately 56W. (5) Thermal limits - Check total losses vs thermal capability. (6) For higher frequencies - Consider MOSFETs for >50kHz. (7) Optimization - Balance switching losses vs filter size. The device is optimized for 5-20kHz typical of motor drive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-20kHz for motor drives; consider thermal limitations above 30kHz.

faqs.question: What applications require 1200V IGBTs?
faqs.answer: 1200V IGBT applications: (1) 690V AC drives - For industrial motors requiring high voltage. (2) Solar inverters - 1000V DC bus commercial systems. (3) EV charging - DC fast charging infrastructure. (4) UPS systems - Large three-phase UPS. (5) Welding - High-power welding equipment. (6) Traction - Electric vehicle motor drives. (7) Renewable energy - Wind power converters. The 1200V rating provides safety margin for 800-1000V DC bus applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1200V IGBTs for 690V AC systems or 1000V DC bus applications.

faqs.question: How does IXGH20N120BD1 compare to 600V IGBTs?
faqs.answer: 1200V vs 600V IGBT comparison: (1) Voltage rating - 2x higher blocking voltage. (2) Vce(sat) - Slightly higher (2.1V vs 1.9V typical). (3) Switching speed - Similar or slightly slower. (4) Cost - Approximately 30-50% higher. (5) Applications - Required for high-voltage systems. (6) Efficiency - Similar at same current density. (7) Safety margin - Higher voltage provides more margin. Choose 1200V only when required by application voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1200V only for high-voltage applications; 600V preferred for 380V systems.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements differ for 1200V IGBTs?
faqs.answer: 1200V IGBT gate drive considerations: (1) Isolation - Higher isolation voltage required (>2500V). (2) dv/dt immunity - Better immunity needed for high voltage switching. (3) Gate resistor - May need higher values to control dv/dt. (4) Miller effect - More pronounced at higher voltage. (5) Dead time - Allow slightly longer dead time. (6) Protection - Desaturation detection essential. (7) Layout - More critical for high voltage. Use gate drivers specifically rated for 1200V IGBTs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1200V-rated gate drivers with adequate isolation and dv/dt immunity.

faqs.question: Can IXGH20N120BD1 be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXGH20N120BD1 is excellent for solar inverters: (1) Voltage rating - 1200V suitable for 1000V DC bus systems. (2) Current rating - 20A supports inverters up to 15kW. (3) Efficiency - Low Vce(sat) for high conversion efficiency. (4) Switching - Fast switching enables high-frequency operation. (5) Reliability - Proven in outdoor solar applications. (6) Diode - Integrated diode simplifies bridge design. (7) Cost-effective - Good performance-to-price ratio. Recommended for commercial string inverters 10-20kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 1000V DC bus solar inverters 10-20kW.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of IXGH20N120BD1?
faqs.answer: IXGH20N120BD1 thermal specifications: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.65°C/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature - 150°C. (3) At 15A with 2.1V Vce(sat): Pcond = 31.5W. (4) Switching losses - Approximately 20W at 20kHz. (5) Total losses - ~50W typical operation. (6) Required heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 1.0°C/W for 50°C ambient. (7) Cooling - Forced air recommended for continuous operation. Thermal design is similar to equivalent 600V devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling; design for Tj < 125°C for long life.

faqs.question: What protection is needed for 1200V IGBT applications?
faqs.answer: Protection for 1200V IGBTs: (1) Overcurrent - Fast desaturation detection <5μs. (2) Overvoltage - Active clamping or TVS for inductive spikes. (3) Undervoltage - Vge monitoring to prevent linear operation. (4) Overtemperature - Heatsink temperature monitoring. (5) Short-circuit - Soft turn-off to limit overvoltage. (6) Gate protection - Voltage clamping to ±20V. (7) Isolation monitoring - For high-voltage safety. 1200V applications require more robust protection due to higher energy levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement comprehensive protection; desaturation detection is essential.

categories.name: Thyristors & Rectifiers
categories.description: IXYS thyristors and rectifiers provide reliable power control and rectification with voltage ratings up to 4500V for industrial and power conversion applications.
categories.longDescription: IXYS is a leading manufacturer of thyristors (SCRs) and rectifier diodes, offering voltage ratings from 200V to 4500V and current ratings up to thousands of amps. The product range includes standard SCRs, fast switching thyristors, phase-control thyristors, and high-voltage rectifier diodes. These devices serve applications in motor soft starters, DC drives, power supplies, welding equipment, and traction systems. IXYS thyristors are known for their high surge current capability, low forward voltage drop, and excellent reliability in demanding industrial environments. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Thyristors & Rectifiers selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.description: High-power thyristors for AC power control

faqs.question: What is the difference between SCR and triac?
faqs.answer: SCR vs triac comparison: (1) Conduction - SCR conducts in one direction, triac bidirectional. (2) Applications - SCR for DC control and half-wave AC, triac for full-wave AC. (3) Quadrants - Triac operates in all four quadrants. (4) Sensitivity - SCR generally more sensitive gate trigger. (5) dv/dt - Triac typically lower dv/dt rating. (6) Commutation - Triac has commutation limitations. (7) Selection - Use SCR for high power DC or half-wave; triac for AC phase control. The MCC95-16IO1B is an SCR for high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SCR for DC or half-wave AC; triac for full-wave AC phase control.

faqs.question: How do I trigger MCC95-16IO1B?
faqs.answer: MCC95-16IO1B triggering requirements: (1) Gate trigger current - 50mA maximum, 15mA typical. (2) Gate trigger voltage - 1.5V maximum. (3) Pulse width - Minimum 10μs for reliable triggering. (4) Gate drive - Use optocoupler or pulse transformer for isolation. (5) Trigger circuit - Provide adequate gate current during turn-on. (6) dv/dt protection - Use snubber circuit to prevent false triggering. (7) Safety - Ensure gate drive is isolated from power circuit. The device is easy to trigger with standard control circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use optically isolated trigger with 20-50mA gate current pulse.

faqs.question: What is surge current rating in thyristors?
faqs.answer: MCC95-16IO1B surge current specifications: (1) Surge current - 1900A for one half-cycle (8.3ms). (2) I²t rating - 15000 A²s for fuse coordination. (3) Application - Handles motor inrush currents. (4) Non-repetitive - Rating for fault conditions. (5) Temperature - Rated at 25°C starting temperature. (6) Protection - Coordinate with fuses or circuit breakers. (7) Design margin - Stay below 50% of rated surge for reliability. The high surge rating makes this device ideal for motor starting applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify surge rating against motor inrush current; coordinate protection devices.

faqs.question: Can MCC95-16IO1B be used for motor soft starters?
faqs.answer: Yes, MCC95-16IO1B is excellent for motor soft starters: (1) Current rating - 95A supports motors up to 45kW. (2) Voltage rating - 1600V suitable for 690V AC systems. (3) Surge capability - Handles 6-8x motor inrush current. (4) Phase control - Smooth voltage ramp reduces starting stress. (5) Reliability - Proven in industrial motor control. (6) Cost-effective - Economical solution for soft starting. (7) Availability - Standard product with good stock. Recommended for three-phase motor soft starters 22-45kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for motor soft starters 22-45kW; use three devices for three-phase.

faqs.question: What is dv/dt rating and why is it important?
faqs.answer: dv/dt considerations for MCC95-16IO1B: (1) Critical dv/dt - 1000V/μs minimum. (2) Static dv/dt - Higher rating when device is off. (3) Commutating dv/dt - Lower rating during turn-off. (4) False triggering - High dv/dt can turn on SCR unintentionally. (5) Snubber circuit - RC network limits dv/dt. (6) Applications - Critical in inductive circuits. (7) Design - Always use snubber for inductive loads. The dv/dt rating ensures reliable operation in switching applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use RC snubber for inductive loads; calculate values based on circuit.

faqs.question: How do I protect thyristors from overcurrent?
faqs.answer: Thyristor overcurrent protection: (1) Fast fuses - Semiconductor fuses with I²t coordination. (2) Circuit breakers - Magnetic only or electronic trip. (3) Current limiting - Reactor or resistor in series. (4) Overcurrent relay - Fast-acting protection relay. (5) I²t coordination - Ensure fuse I²t < device I²t. (6) Thermal protection - Heatsink overtemperature switch. (7) Gate protection - Prevent gate overvoltage. Proper protection is essential as thyristors have limited overcurrent capability compared to fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use semiconductor fuses with proper I²t coordination; implement fast overcurrent detection.

faqs.question: What is reverse recovery time in fast recovery diodes?
faqs.answer: Reverse recovery time explained: (1) Definition - Time for diode to switch from forward to reverse blocking. (2) DSEI60-12A - 35ns typical recovery time. (3) Soft recovery - Low EMI and voltage overshoot. (4) Hard recovery - Fast but higher EMI. (5) Switching loss - Proportional to recovery time and frequency. (6) Applications - Critical in high-frequency converters. (7) Selection - Choose based on operating frequency. Fast recovery reduces losses but may cost more than standard diodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use fast recovery diodes for frequencies above 10kHz; standard diodes for line frequency.

faqs.question: How do I select a rectifier diode for my application?
faqs.answer: Rectifier diode selection criteria: (1) Voltage rating - PIV > 2.5 × maximum reverse voltage. (2) Current rating - IF(AV) > 1.5 × average forward current. (3) Recovery time - Match to switching frequency. (4) Forward voltage - Lower Vf reduces conduction loss. (5) Surge current - Must handle inrush currents. (6) Package - Thermal requirements determine package. (7) Cost - Balance performance and price. For DSEI60-12A: 1200V, 60A, 35ns - suitable for high-frequency applications up to 100kHz.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage and current with safety margin; match recovery time to frequency.

faqs.question: Can DSEI60-12A be used as a freewheeling diode?
faqs.answer: Yes, DSEI60-12A is excellent for freewheeling applications: (1) Fast recovery - 35ns minimizes switching loss. (2) Voltage rating - 1200V suitable for high-voltage inverters. (3) Current rating - 60A handles motor freewheeling currents. (4) Soft recovery - Low EMI in motor drive applications. (5) Ruggedness - High surge current capability. (6) Temperature - Reliable operation up to 150°C. (7) Cost-effective - Good performance-to-price ratio. Recommended for IGBT/MOSFET freewheeling in motor drives and inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for high-frequency freewheeling applications; match voltage to switching device.

faqs.question: What is the difference between standard and fast recovery diodes?
faqs.answer: Standard vs fast recovery comparison: (1) Recovery time - Standard: 1-10μs, Fast: 25-100ns. (2) Frequency - Standard for <1kHz, Fast for >10kHz. (3) Switching loss - Fast diodes have much lower switching loss. (4) Forward voltage - Standard often lower Vf. (5) Cost - Fast diodes typically 2-3x more expensive. (6) Applications - Standard for line rectification, Fast for SMPS. (7) EMI - Fast diodes may generate more EMI. Choose based on operating frequency and efficiency requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard diodes for 50/60Hz; fast recovery for switching power supplies.

faqs.question: How do I parallel rectifier diodes for higher current?
faqs.answer: Paralleling diode guidelines: (1) Current sharing - Use small series resistors (0.01-0.1Ω). (2) Matching - Select diodes from same batch for similar Vf. (3) Layout - Symmetrical layout with equal trace lengths. (4) Thermal - Common heatsink for temperature matching. (5) Derating - Use 80% of combined rating. (6) Imbalance - Expect 10-20% current variation. (7) Testing - Verify sharing under load. With proper design, two DSEI60-12A can handle 100A continuous current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use current sharing resistors; match devices; verify sharing under load.

faqs.question: What causes diode failure and how can it be prevented?
faqs.answer: Common diode failure modes: (1) Overvoltage - Exceeding PIV rating causes avalanche. (2) Overcurrent - Excessive forward current overheats junction. (3) Thermal runaway - Inadequate cooling increases temperature. (4) dv/dt failure - Excessive rate of voltage change. (5) Mechanical - Thermal cycling causes package stress. (6) Prevention - Proper voltage/current derating, adequate cooling, surge protection. (7) Reliability - Keep Tj below 125°C for long life. Follow datasheet recommendations for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Derate voltage and current; ensure adequate cooling; implement protection.

categories.name: Power Modules
categories.description: IXYS power modules integrate multiple power devices for complete power conversion solutions in motor drives, inverters, and power supplies.
categories.longDescription: IXYS power modules combine multiple power semiconductors in compact packages for high-power applications. The module portfolio includes IGBT modules, MOSFET modules, thyristor modules, and rectifier modules. These integrated solutions reduce design complexity, improve reliability, and minimize system size. IXYS modules feature advanced thermal management, low-inductance design, and comprehensive protection features. Applications include industrial motor drives, renewable energy systems, traction drives, and high-power power supplies. Voltage ratings range from 600V to 1700V with current ratings up to hundreds of amps. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: IGBT Modules
series.description: Half-bridge and full-bridge IGBT modules for motor drives

selectionGuide.title: How to Select IXYS Power Modules
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting power modules for your application

products.shortDescription: High-quality power modules component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.

Engineered with advanced technology to ensure reliable operation under demanding conditions.
Suitable for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Excellent power modules component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of using power modules vs discrete devices?
faqs.answer: Power module advantages: (1) Integration - Multiple devices in one package. (2) Low inductance - Minimized stray inductance reduces voltage spikes. (3) Thermal management - Optimized heat spreading. (4) Reliability - Factory-tested and matched devices. (5) Design time - Faster time-to-market. (6) Size - Compact compared to discrete solutions. (7) Cost - Lower system cost despite higher module price. Modules are ideal for production designs where reliability and time-to-market are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use modules for production designs; discrete for prototyping or low volume.

faqs.question: How do I mount and cool IXYS power modules?
faqs.answer: Module mounting guidelines: (1) Heatsink surface - Flatness within 0.05mm, roughness 3.2μm. (2) Thermal interface - Use thermal grease or phase-change material. (3) Mounting torque - Follow datasheet specifications exactly. (4) Mounting sequence - Gradual tightening in diagonal pattern. (5) Thermal resistance - Calculate based on Rth(j-c) and Rth(c-s). (6) Airflow - Ensure adequate cooling airflow. (7) Temperature monitoring - Use built-in NTC if available. Proper mounting is critical for reliable thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet mounting instructions; use proper thermal interface material.

faqs.question: What protection features are built into MUBW15-12A7?
faqs.answer: MUBW15-12A7 protection features: (1) Undervoltage lockout - Disables operation at low Vge. (2) Overcurrent protection - Desaturation detection and soft shutdown. (3) Overtemperature protection - Module shutdown at high temperature. (4) Short-circuit protection - Fast detection and shutdown. (5) Active Miller clamp - Prevents false turn-on. (6) Fault output - Digital fault indication. (7) Isolation - 2500Vrms isolation rating. These protections significantly improve system reliability and reduce external component count.
faqs.decisionGuide: All protections active by default; monitor fault output for diagnostics.

faqs.question: Can MUBW15-12A7 be used for servo drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, MUBW15-12A7 is suitable for servo drives: (1) Current rating - 15A supports servos up to 2kW. (2) Voltage rating - 1200V suitable for 380V servo systems. (3) Switching frequency - Supports up to 20kHz carrier. (4) Protection - Comprehensive protection for reliable operation. (5) Integration - Reduces design complexity. (6) Size - Compact for servo drive enclosures. (7) Cost-effective - Good value for integrated solution. Recommended for small servo drives and general-purpose inverters up to 2kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for servo drives up to 2kW; consider higher current modules for larger servos.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency limitation of MUBW15-12A7?
faqs.answer: MUBW15-12A7 frequency characteristics: (1) Maximum frequency - 25kHz practical limit. (2) Recommended range - 5-16kHz for motor drives. (3) Switching losses - Increase proportionally with frequency. (4) Thermal limits - Check total losses at chosen frequency. (5) Efficiency - Higher frequency reduces efficiency. (6) EMI - Higher frequency increases EMI. (7) Optimization - Balance switching frequency vs efficiency. The module is optimized for standard motor drive frequencies of 5-16kHz.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-16kHz for motor drives; verify thermal performance at higher frequencies.

faqs.question: How do I interface MUBW15-12A7 with my controller?
faqs.answer: Controller interface for MUBW15-12A7: (1) Control inputs - Logic-level PWM inputs (3.3V/5V). (2) Input isolation - Built-in optocoupler isolation. (3) Dead time - Insert 2-5μs dead time in controller. (4) Fault output - Open-collector fault signal. (5) Enable input - Active-high enable control. (6) Temperature output - Analog temperature signal. (7) Power supplies - +15V for high-side, +15V for low-side. Interface is straightforward with standard MCU PWM outputs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect PWM outputs directly; implement dead time in software; monitor fault output.

faqs.question: What is a three-phase rectifier bridge?
faqs.answer: Three-phase rectifier bridge explained: (1) Configuration - Six diodes in bridge arrangement. (2) Function - Converts three-phase AC to DC. (3) Output - Six-pulse DC with lower ripple than single-phase. (4) Efficiency - Higher than single-phase for same power. (5) Applications - Industrial power supplies, motor drives. (6) Advantages - Balanced loading, lower ripple, higher power. (7) Module form - Integrated six-diode bridge in one package. The VUO82-16NO7 provides complete three-phase rectification in a compact module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use three-phase rectifier for industrial applications above 5kW.

faqs.question: How do I calculate rectifier output voltage and current?
faqs.answer: Three-phase rectifier calculations: (1) DC output voltage - Vdc = 1.35 × VLL (line-line AC voltage). For 380V AC: Vdc = 513V. (2) DC current - Idc = P / Vdc. For 30kW: Idc = 58A. (3) Diode current - Average If = Idc / 3 = 19.3A. (4) Diode PIV - Must exceed peak line voltage: 1.414 × 380V = 537V. (5) Ripple frequency - 6 × line frequency = 300Hz (50Hz) or 360Hz (60Hz). (6) Power factor - Approximately 0.95 for six-pulse. (7) Efficiency - Typically 98-99% for diode rectifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate DC voltage as 1.35 × AC line voltage; ensure diode ratings have margin.

faqs.question: What input protection is needed for rectifier modules?
faqs.answer: Rectifier input protection: (1) Fuses - Semiconductor fuses on AC input lines. (2) Surge protection - MOVs or TVS for voltage transients. (3) Inrush limiting - NTC thermistors or resistors with bypass. (4) EMI filter - For conducted emissions reduction. (5) Overvoltage - Protection against line surges. (6) Undervoltage - Protection for low line conditions. (7) Phase loss - Detection for three-phase imbalance. Proper protection ensures reliable operation and compliance with safety standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement fuses, surge protection, and inrush limiting as minimum protection.

faqs.question: Can VUO82-16NO7 be used for battery charger applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, VUO82-16NO7 is excellent for battery chargers: (1) Current rating - 82A supports chargers up to 40kW. (2) Voltage rating - 1600V suitable for high-voltage battery systems. (3) Efficiency - Low forward voltage minimizes losses. (4) Surge capability - Handles charging inrush currents. (5) Reliability - Proven in industrial charger applications. (6) Three-phase - Balanced loading on AC supply. (7) Cost-effective - Integrated solution reduces BOM cost. Recommended for industrial battery chargers and EV charging infrastructure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for three-phase battery chargers 15-40kW; use with proper filtering.

faqs.question: What is the difference between half-wave and full-wave rectification?
faqs.answer: Half-wave vs full-wave comparison: (1) Half-wave - Uses one diode, conducts on half cycle only. (2) Full-wave - Uses multiple diodes, conducts both half cycles. (3) Efficiency - Full-wave 2x higher than half-wave. (4) Ripple - Full-wave has 2x frequency, lower amplitude ripple. (5) Transformer - Full-wave requires center tap or bridge. (6) Diode stress - Half-wave: higher PIV, lower average current. (7) Applications - Half-wave for low power, full-wave for most applications. Three-phase bridge provides six-pulse full-wave rectification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use full-wave rectification for all but lowest power applications.

faqs.question: How do I size the DC link capacitor for a rectifier?
faqs.answer: DC link capacitor sizing: (1) Ripple voltage - Determine acceptable Vpp ripple. (2) Capacitance - C = Idc / (2 × f × Vripple) for single-phase, C = Idc / (6 × f × Vripple) for three-phase. (3) Example - For 50A, 5V ripple, 50Hz: C = 50 / (6 × 50 × 5) = 33,000μF. (4) Voltage rating - 1.5 × maximum DC voltage. (5) RMS current - Must handle ripple current. (6) ESR - Lower ESR reduces heating. (7) Life - Higher temperature reduces capacitor life. Size for both capacitance and ripple current requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate capacitance based on ripple requirement; verify ripple current rating.
